parts.text:
1. SettingRow — the titled bordered card with a control on the right (flex items-start justify-between gap-4 rounded-lg border border-border p-4 → title +
  description + slot). 6 instances (KeatingUiSettings, Speech). Pairs cleanly with Toggle. Good win.
  2. SettingsSectionHeader — h3 + muted p. 11 instances, but trivial markup with varying margins; low payoff.
  3. Radio card group — 7-ish instances, but they diverge (Speech adds status badges + API-key hints, ModelSelector differs), so only a partial/awkward extract.

  My recommendation: do SettingRow (real duplication, low risk), skip the header (not worth the indirection), and leave the radio groups as-is (too
  heterogeneous).

Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify the exact SettingRow instances to extract:

These are the classic `flex items-start justify-between gap-4 rounded-lg border border-border p-4` with text left and Toggle right.

- `title: string` — the bold text
- `description?: string | ReactNode` — the muted description paragraph
- `children: ReactNode` — the control on the right
- `className?: string` — for extra classes like `scroll-mt-20`
- `id?: string` — for anchor links

This is a very simple component. I'll also consider whether the left side should allow custom content or just title+description. Looking at the patterns, all 6 instances have just title + description on the left. Let me keep it simple.

**New component:** `web/src/components/SettingRow.tsx`
- Simple card layout: `title` (bold) + optional `description` (muted) on the left, `children` (control slot) on the right
- Supports `id` (for anchor scrolling) and `className` (for utility classes like `scroll-mt-20`)

**6 instances replaced:**

| File | Setting |
|---|---|
| `KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx` | Show tool details |

| `SpeechSettingsTab.tsx` | Enable spoken responses |
| `SpeechSettingsTab.tsx` | Microphone (duplex providers) |

**Skipped as planned:**
- **SettingsSectionHeader** — 11 instances left inline (trivial markup, varying margins)
- **Radio card groups** — left heterogeneous (status badges, API-key hints, delete buttons, selected-state styling)
- **ProvidersModelsTab** variants — `p-3` padding, no-description rows, and delete-button rows left inline since they diverge from the canonical pattern

- `bun run build` (vite + nitro) — clean success
